title: Why We're Here
confidence_notes: Church-plant vision sermon on the Great Commission. Dominant emphasis is Jesus' authority and sending the church to make disciples, with substantial attention to baptism as identity/naming in the triune name.

title: Who We Are
confidence_notes: Introductory Ephesians sermon centers on identity in Christ as saints and children of God, with significant attention to the church, God's word and will, gospel advance from Ephesus, and a concluding Lord's Supper application.

title: Blessed in Christ (Pt. 1)
confidence_notes: Ephesians 1 sermon surveys the whole arc of spiritual blessing in Christ: election, adoption, redemption, inheritance, sealing by the Spirit, and receiving these blessings by faith.

title: Blessed in Christ (Pt. 2)
confidence_notes: The sermon explicitly frames redemption as planned by the Father, accomplished by the Son, and applied by the Spirit, emphasizing God's sovereign plan, election, Christ's redeeming work, sealing by the Spirit, and praise to God's glory.

title: Knowing God
confidence_notes: Exposition of Paul's prayer that believers would know God more deeply by the Spirit, especially through hope, inheritance, and resurrection/ascension power in Christ for the church.

title: Understanding Your Spiritual Condition
confidence_notes: Guest sermon focuses on the unbeliever's dead spiritual condition, sin, worldliness, Satan's influence, God's mercy in making sinners alive, the call to personal faith, and the eternal stakes of judgment and hell. Added sanctification per the original-sin/fallen-condition disambiguation rule.

title: Saved by Grace
confidence_notes: Clear sermon on salvation by grace through faith, contrasting spiritual death with new life in Christ and explaining good works as fruit rather than root of salvation.

title: A New Humanity
confidence_notes: Sermon centers on Christ creating one new humanity from Jew and Gentile, reconciling sinners to God and one another through the cross, with sustained attention to covenant inclusion, the law's fulfilled role, gospel peace, and multiethnic church witness.

title: A Living Temple
confidence_notes: The sermon develops Paul's metaphors of citizens, household members, and living temple to ground the church's given identity, access to the Father, Christ as cornerstone/tabernacling presence, the Spirit's indwelling, Abrahamic promise fulfillment, and secure position in grace.

title: The Wondrous Mystery
confidence_notes: Dominant theme is the revealed mystery that Jew and Gentile are one body in Christ through the gospel, displayed in the global and local church through apostolic Scripture and gospel ministry.

title: An Unimpressive Tree
confidence_notes: Advent sermon reads Isaiah's servant as the unimpressive, suffering Messiah whose incarnation, substitutionary death, resurrection, and gospel fruit call hearers to believe and rejoice.

title: Life Together
confidence_notes: Life Together focuses on the church's unity, shared ministry, and maturity in Christ, grounded in the triune God, Spirit-given gifts, leaders equipping saints through the word, and the body growing in Christlike holiness.

title: The New Self
confidence_notes: The sermon contrasts the old self and new self, warning against worldly futility, deceitful desires, impurity, and hardness of heart while calling hearers to put on the new self, learn Christ, renew the mind, and live as new creations.

title: Walk in Love
confidence_notes: Practical sermon on putting off the old self and putting on Christlike habits: truthfulness, restrained anger, forgiveness, honest work, generosity, and gracious speech by the Spirit.

title: Walk in the Light
confidence_notes: Practical holiness sermon on living as children of light: rejecting sexual immorality, covetousness, and corrupt speech, replacing darkness with thankful obedience and public witness.

title: A Profound Mystery
confidence_notes: Marriage text is preached as a profound mystery pointing to Christ and the church, with major attention to wives and husbands, Christlike servant leadership, submission, Christ's sacrificial and purifying love, and practical discipleship in marriage.

title: Christ at Home
confidence_notes: Sermon applies Ephesians to children and parents, emphasizing children's obedience and honor, parental nurture and discipline, family discipleship, gospel grace for homes, God's fatherly love and adoption, and the church's welcome of children.

title: Christ at Work
confidence_notes: Sermon applies Christ's lordship to everyday work, treating vocation as discipleship and worship while addressing slavery, dignity, workplace justice, motives, relationships, and Christ's servant work.

title: The Advance of the Gospel
confidence_notes: Final Ephesians sermon centers on the gospel advancing through wartime prayer and mission partnership, with the church standing firm in spiritual battle, praying in the Spirit, supporting gospel workers, and boldly proclaiming the mystery of the gospel locally and globally.

title: Jesus Is
confidence_notes: Series opener explains Jesus' absolute I AM claim, John's eyewitness purpose, signs, cross and resurrection, and the call to believe and follow. Gospel and Scripture tags are included because the sermon explicitly explains John's written witness and the gospel response.

title: I Am the Light of the World
confidence_notes: The sermon centers on Jesus as divine light, giver of sight and life, and fulfillment of Old Testament light imagery. It applies the healed blind man's faith to discipleship, confession of sin, public witness, and the believer's identity as children of light.

title: I Am the Good Shepherd
confidence_notes: Jesus is preached as the promised shepherd-king, the door, and the good shepherd who lays down his life for the sheep. The sermon gives sustained attention to his effectual call, secure keeping of his sheep, sovereign care, and the gathering of sheep from beyond Israel.

title: I Am the Resurrection and the Life
confidence_notes: Easter sermon centers on Jesus as the resurrection and life, comforting grief with his love, death, resurrection power, promise of final bodily resurrection, and call to believe.

title: I Am the Way, the Truth, and the Life
confidence_notes: The sermon focuses on Jesus as the exclusive way to the Father and comfort in death, with significant teaching on the intermediate state, bodily resurrection, Christ's return, the Father's house, and Jesus preparing a place through his death and resurrection.

title: I Am the Vine
confidence_notes: Fruitfulness, abiding, pruning, obedience, and growth in Christ dominate the sermon. Jesus is the true vine/source of life, the Father is the gardener, and practical abiding is developed through the word, prayer, dependence, and persevering faith.

title: Shaped by the Shema
confidence_notes: The sermon expounds the Shema as attentiveness to the one true God, wholehearted love and worship, and a life shaped by God's word. It strongly applies Deuteronomy 6 to family discipleship, speaking of God at home, and public witness.

title: Blessed is the Man
confidence_notes: Psalm 1 opens the Psalms as a path of blessing: delighting in God's Word, refusing the world's counsel, growing in fruitfulness, and finding true righteousness and blessing in Christ.

title: Christ Our King
confidence_notes: Psalm 2 is preached as the story of raging nations, God's unshaken reign, and the appointed Son/King. The sermon emphasizes Christ's just and merciful kingship, cosmic conflict, final victory, judgment, and hope for believers living under spiritual opposition.

title: Save Me, O God
confidence_notes: The sermon treats Psalm 3 as a cry for help amid enemies, fear, persecution, and despair. It highlights naming trouble in prayer, remembering God's character, resting in his sustaining care, and seeing ultimate salvation through Christ's suffering, resurrection, and reign.

title: Dwelling in Safety
confidence_notes: Psalm 4 is preached as movement from distress to rest through prayer, remembering God's righteous, gracious, faithful care, trusting one's set-apart status as God's child, refusing sinful anger, and seeking God's face for joy and peace.

title: Lead Me in Your Righteousness
confidence_notes: The sermon centers on praying to be led in God's righteousness under God's reign. It develops God's holiness, the deadliness and deceitfulness of sin, lying speech, wrath, refuge in God's steadfast love, and Christ as sacrifice, righteousness, and refuge.

title: Beauty Through Brokenness
confidence_notes: Psalm 6 is preached as lament and penitential prayer: bringing tears, distress, grief, and possible guilt to the God of mercy, with hope grounded in Christ's suffering and deliverance.

title: The Righteousness of God
confidence_notes: Psalm 7 is preached around God as righteous judge: he cares about injustice and false accusation, delivers the righteous in Christ, calls sinners to repent, and will bring final justice.

title: Strength Through Weakness
confidence_notes: Psalm 8 is read in light of creation, human dignity, weakness, God's mindful care, and Christ as the true Son of Man who lowers himself through suffering and death. Prayer and worship are secondary but sustained through the theme of weak cries becoming praise.

title: Defiant Praise
confidence_notes: The sermon centers on praising God with integrity while still in battle because he is supreme, just, a stronghold for the oppressed, and the coming Savior. Judgment and salvation are included because the sermon repeatedly contrasts God's judgment on enemies with rescue for the needy.

title: An Attentive God in Troubling Times
confidence_notes: Psalm 10 sermon laments a troubling world of pride, oppression, and practical atheism, directing hearers to pray to the God who sees, hears, strengthens, and takes on evil in Christ.

title: The Holiness of Hunkering Down
confidence_notes: Psalm 11 is preached as a call to resist fear-driven retreat, stand firm because God reigns, and trust that trials refine the righteous. Cultural witness is secondary but substantial in the application about cultural panic, shifting trends, and remaining faithful.

title: The Holiness of Hunkerin'
confidence_notes: Psalm 11 sermon on holy 'hunkering down' — taking refuge in the Lord when the foundations are shaking. Three reasons for holy hunkering: (1) the Lord is in his holy temple, his throne in heaven — he hasn't changed address, so he is the bedrock stability for shifting cultural sands; (2) God uses troubles to test (his eyes see, his eyelids scrutinize), refining the righteous like gold in a craftsman's fire and growing them in steadfastness; (3) God promises something better — the upright shall behold his face, a future of righteousness in the new creation that makes every trial worth it. Closes at the Lord's table, framing communion as both looking back to the cross and forward to the day we see him face to face. Topics chosen to capture the God's unchanging character, the refining purpose of trials, the covenant hope, the present call to repentance and faith, the sanctifying work of the Spirit, and the worship context of the Lord's Supper.

title: The Faithful and the Wicked
confidence_notes: The sermon focuses on David's cry for help, wicked speech, flattering lips, boasting, the purity of God's words, and God's protection of the poor and needy. Salvation is included because the sermon ends with waiting for the God who saves and pleads the sinner's cause.

title: A Life of Deep Faith
confidence_notes: Psalm 13 is preached as lament moving from honest anguish to prayerful request and trust in God's steadfast love. Community is included because the sermon gives extended application to brothers, sisters, and home groups helping believers remember and pray when they cannot.

title: The Short-Sighted Fool
confidence_notes: The sermon expounds the fool's practical atheism, universal corruption, and need to call on the Lord, then contrasts that with the righteous who welcome Christ, know God's presence, and hope in salvation. Assurance and judgment are secondary but clear in the final warnings and promises.

title: One Voice: Glorifying God through Our Worship
confidence_notes: Series opener on the church glorifying God with one voice through worship, grounding worship in God's revealed glory, Scripture, Christ, shared harmony, and whole-life service.

title: One Voice: Glorifying God Through Our Welcome
confidence_notes: This One Voice sermon applies Romans 15 to the church's welcome of one another across differences, grounded in Christ's welcome through the gospel. Christian liberty is included because the sermon works from Romans 14-15 disputes over food, days, and conscience matters.

title: One Voice: Glorifying God Through Our Witness
confidence_notes: The sermon is a direct call to understand and engage global mission through Paul's priestly gospel ministry, word-and-deed witness, unreached places, sending, prayer, and partnership. Mercy/justice is secondary because the Jerusalem collection and practical support for saints are treated as part of mission.

title: Birth 2.0
confidence_notes: The sermon centers on the necessity, nature, and reception of the new birth: sins washed away, a new heart by the Spirit, and faith looking to Christ lifted up. Sanctification and life after death are included because the sermon closes with living out new life and hope in the coming kingdom.

title: I Will Build My Church
confidence_notes: Anniversary/church-plant sermon centered on Jesus' promise to build his church, grounding confidence in his identity, cross-and-resurrection work, and the shared call to follow him together.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About God
confidence_notes: Series sermon on Trinity's statement about God. Paul at Athens frames God as creator, sustainer, ruler, near, Father, judge, and Savior, with cultural witness and gospel proclamation prominent.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Scripture
confidence_notes: Doctrine of Scripture sermon: canon, inspiration, authority, sufficiency, Christ-centered revelation, Scripture's saving purpose, and its role in training believers for transformation and good works.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Humanity
confidence_notes: Humanity/image-of-God sermon fits the creation anchor: humans as creatures, embodied image bearers, male and female, made to reflect God's glory, cultivate creation, and have the image restored in Christ.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Sin
confidence_notes: Doctrine-of-sin sermon covering sin as missing God's glory, sinful acts/desires/nature, original sin in Adam, and Christ as the second Adam whose righteousness and life are received by faith.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About Jesus
confidence_notes: Jesus doctrine sermon emphasizes the incarnate Word as fully God and fully human, Son from the Father, center of Scripture, crucified and raised, and personally received by faith for life.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Salvation
confidence_notes: Doctrine of Salvation sermon on John 3:16-21. Walks through four questions (why saved, who saves, how know, what saved for): we are perishing under God's wrath; the triune God saves — Father sending, Son descending and being lifted up on the cross, Spirit giving new birth; received by grace alone through faith alone; and salvation is past (justified), present (eternal life as living union with Christ), and future (glorification). Topics chosen to capture the trinitarian shape of salvation, the finished work of Christ, the instrumentality of faith, and the present assurance believers have.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About the Holy Spirit
confidence_notes: Holy Spirit sermon on Pentecost, the Spirit's deity/personhood, indwelling presence, regeneration, transformation, gifts for the church, witness to Christ, and communion as fellowship by the Spirit.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About the Church
confidence_notes: First church sermon focuses on identity: the church as community, family, temple, and kingdom outpost, created for fellowship, gathered by the gospel, indwelt by the Spirit, and witnessing to Christ's reign.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Church Part I
confidence_notes: Doctrine of the Church Part I focuses on the identity of the church from Acts 1-2 and the word 'they' in Acts 2:42. Four images: community (created for and reconciled into), family (Jesus defines family by doing the Father's will — the most common NT metaphor), temple (God's Spirit dwells in his people corporately), and kingdom outpost (a community that confesses and lives under King Jesus). Closes with baptism as the outward sign of inclusion. Topics chosen to surface the church's identity, communal life, Spirit's presence, the ordinance of baptism, and the church's worship and witness.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About the Church
confidence_notes: Second church sermon focuses on practices: receiving the gospel, baptism, apostles' teaching, fellowship, breaking bread, prayers, shared life in homes, generosity, and common witness as the Lord adds to the church.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Church Part II
confidence_notes: Doctrine of the Church Part II focuses on the practices of the church from Acts 2:42 — the ordinary means of grace: the apostles' teaching (Scripture as the church's authority), fellowship (interdependent care and shared life), the breaking of bread (the Lord's Supper), and prayers. Treats baptism as the doorway into the church and the Lord's Supper as the family table the Lord has given. Emphasizes the Spirit's work in producing these practices, the devotion of the early believers, and the common witness that flows outward as the Lord adds to their number day by day.

title: Joyful Surrender
confidence_notes: Advent joy sermon on the treasure hidden in the field: seeing Christ and his kingdom as supremely valuable, joyfully surrendering lesser treasures, and letting that joy reshape giving, obedience, and worship.

title: Why Bethlehem?
confidence_notes: Christmas sermon on why Christ was born in Bethlehem: prophecy, God's providence, Christ's identity as eternal king, and the Savior's rescue mission leading to worship and hope beyond death.

title: The Exodus of Jesus
confidence_notes: Series opener reads Mark 1 as the climactic Exodus pattern fulfilled in Jesus: baptism as death/resurrection and judgment/salvation, wilderness obedience, Sonship, kingdom proclamation, and the call to repent and believe the gospel.

title: Blessing and Opposition
confidence_notes: Exodus opening sermon emphasizes God's blessing and purposes prevailing under Pharaoh's opposition, creation/fruitfulness echoes, courageous God-fearing witness by the midwives and Moses' family, and the Redeemer pattern fulfilled in Christ.

title: God Is at Work Even Here
confidence_notes: Sermon centers on God working unseen through Moses' formation, failure, exile, and Israel's groaning; God hears, remembers covenant, sees, knows, refines Moses, and the adoption illustration receives enough doctrinal reflection to tag.

title: The Burning Bush
confidence_notes: Exodus sermon on God's self-revelation as the holy, self-existent I AM who sees suffering, speaks, reveals his name and plan, and sends Moses while supplying presence, promises, and power.

title: Delay, Disillusionment, and the Faithfulness of God
confidence_notes: Sermon addresses worship followed by worse hardship, grounding patience and prayer in God's fatherly care, Israel as firstborn son, covenant faithfulness, adoption in Christ, and the security of God's promises despite disillusionment.

title: The Plagues
confidence_notes: Plagues sermon presents Yahweh's supreme rule and judgment over Pharaoh, Egypt, false gods, and creation itself; it also develops hardening/providence, Israel's distinct witness, salvation through judgment, and Christ bearing judgment on the cross.

title: The Passover
confidence_notes: Passover sermon focuses on redemption by the blood of the lamb, God's righteous judgment and mercy, Christ as the spotless Passover Lamb, faith in his sufficient sacrifice, and the Lord's Supper as the new covenant remembrance.

title: The Red Sea
confidence_notes: Red Sea sermon begins with fear when trapped, then calls hearers to stand firm and see the Lord's salvation; it develops God's guiding providence, his fight against enemies, Christ's death as judgment-bearing salvation, baptism imagery, and hope in trials.

title: I Am and I Will
confidence_notes: Song of Moses sermon centers on worship as the fitting response to Yahweh's fulfilled promises, victory, supremacy, redemption, and eternal reign, then explicitly reads those themes as fulfilled in Christ and his gospel.

title: Wilderness Training
confidence_notes: Wilderness testing is presented as God's training ground for transformation, dependence, and obedience; the sermon stresses God's gracious provision in hardship, Jesus as the true bread, and the Lord's Supper as the church's wilderness remembrance.

title: Water from the Rock
confidence_notes: The sermon follows Meribah, Massah, and mercy: quarreling exposes sinful desires, testing God reveals distrust in suffering, Moses cries to the Lord, and the struck rock points to Christ struck in judgment so living water may flow.

title: Striving Together
confidence_notes: Exodus 17-18 is applied to the church striving together in the Lord's strength, with Moses' intercession, Jethro's conversion, mission to the nations, shared ministry, elder plurality, home groups, and every-member participation all receiving significant attention.

title: Risen Indeed
confidence_notes: Easter sermon on the Emmaus road emphasizes the risen Christ drawing near, his necessary suffering and resurrection, Christ-centered interpretation of all Scripture, renewed hope, and the disciples' worshipful witness that the Lord is risen indeed.

title: Mount Sinai: Call and Commission
confidence_notes: Sinai's calling is applied through 1 Peter 2 to the church: God saves and brings his people to himself, names them treasured possession, royal priesthood, and holy nation, commissions them to proclaim his excellencies, and calls for obedience to his authoritative word.

title: Let Us Draw Near
confidence_notes: Exodus 24 is preached as covenant confirmation and communion: God's word is central in worship, blood atones and points to Christ's better covenant, the elders behold and eat before God, and believers draw near through Christ's mediation and at the Lord's table.

title: The Golden Calf
confidence_notes: Exodus 32 sermon on Israel's idolatry with the golden calf. Defines idolatry as trusting created things rather than the Creator for hope, happiness, significance, and security, and shows it is cultivated over time (Israel fell back to surrounding cultural patterns). Then turns to God's response — Moses's two petitions reveal God as both merciful (relenting from judgment because of his name and his covenant) and just (the Levites' sword, Moses denied as mediator). Where do we see Jesus? Moses is an imperfect intercessor whose life cannot atone; only Christ, the spotless Lamb, drinks the cup of God's wrath in our place and preserves both God's mercy and his justice. Topics chosen to surface the sin of idolatry (tagged temptation-and-sin), the character of God, the atoning work of Christ, the law/gospel contrast, God's sovereign mercy, and the covenant context.

title: Show Me Your Glory
confidence_notes: Moses' request to see God's glory drives the sermon, with God's self-revelation as merciful, gracious, just, and covenantally faithful leading to prayer, worship, covenant renewal, and the claim that God's glory is most fully seen in Jesus and the cross.

title: From Glory to Glory
confidence_notes: Dual-text sermon uses Moses' shining face and Paul's exposition to contrast old and new covenant glory, center the unveiled vision of Christ, and explain Spirit-wrought transformation from one degree of glory to another while distinguishing sanctification from justification.

title: Life Together
confidence_notes: Joint-service sermon on Paul's closing blessing centers on the triune God whose grace, love, and fellowship create the church's shared life; it applies that to family identity, holiness, restoration, encouragement, unity, embodied love, and Christ's reconciling work.

title: The Twelve Spies
confidence_notes: Numbers 13-14 is preached as a warning against unbelief and fear and a call to ongoing faith in the Lord who saves, keeps, and fulfills his promises. Local inventory describes this as continuing the series through Numbers; no taxonomy gap found.

title: Who Can Dwell with God?
confidence_notes: Psalm 15 sermon centers on who may dwell with the holy God: the perfect integrity fulfilled in Christ, imputed righteousness through union with him, and Spirit-formed integrity in Christian life.

title: Life, Joy and Pleasure
confidence_notes: Psalm 16 is preached as single-minded refuge in the Lord, who is the believer's portion, counselor, sovereign keeper, and source of life, joy, and pleasure. Anxiety/fear/hope is prominent in the contrast between being shaken and setting the Lord before us; Christ's resurrection and eternal security are explicit through Acts 2, with repentance included as the closing call to turn from functional idols back to Jesus.
